title: Lineage Canvas — Lineage Extract (v1.0)
description: The ONLY accepted shape for a lineage extract produced from a SAS/SQL script and loaded via the app's 'Upload JSON' button. The agent must output a SINGLE JSON object that validates against this schema and NOTHING else (no Markdown, no prose, no code fences). NUANCES THAT APPLY TO THE WHOLE DOCUMENT: (1) Import is strictly ADDITIVE and NON-DESTRUCTIVE — it can create new tables, append new columns, and add connections, but it NEVER overwrites existing table/column metadata and NEVER deletes tables, columns, or connections. So this format has no 'delete'/'rename existing' semantics; only describe what should EXIST. (2) IDENTITY: every table is identified by its `dataset_id`, which MUST be `<SYSTEM>:<NAMESPACE>.<NAME>` in UPPERCASE (e.g. 'LEGACY:SALES.CUSTOMERS'). To MERGE into a table that already exists you must reuse its EXACT system/namespace/name from the inventory you were given; any difference creates a separate (duplicate) table instead. Internally the app prefixes this with the canvas id, so you only ever provide the `<SYSTEM>:<NAMESPACE>.<NAME>` part. (3) UPPERCASE everything that is an identifier: system, namespace, table name, column names, and every dataset_id/column referenced in processes and edges — existing objects are stored uppercase, so matching avoids duplicates. (4) `edge_id` values are REQUIRED by this schema but are IGNORED for storage identity — the app derives its own content-based id from the endpoints, so re-importing the same lineage upserts (no duplicate connections); you do not need stable edge ids across runs. (5) All four arrays (datasets, processes, table_edges, column_edges) are optional — emit only what the script actually evidences. A table referenced by an edge but omitted from `datasets` is auto-created as a minimal STUB node. (6) Prefer HIGH-signal, defensible lineage; when a transformation or source cannot be determined, use transformation_type 'UNKNOWN' rather than guessing.

schema_version.description: Schema version. MUST be exactly the string "1.0". Any other value is rejected by the importer.

extract.description: Provenance for this extraction run (one per uploaded file).

extract_id.description: A unique id for this extraction run. Any non-empty string (e.g. the script name + an ISO timestamp).

source_system.description: The PRIMARY system the script belongs to. A SAS script is the LEGACY side → use 'LEGACY'. Use 'TARGET' only if the file is the migration target (e.g. Snowflake SQL). Note: individual datasets can still set their own `system` (a SAS script may create a TARGET-side structure), this field is just the file's default/primary system.

source_file_name.description: The originating file name. RECOMMENDED to make it unique per run (e.g. append a timestamp like 'build_summary.sas@2026-06-20T10:00:00Z') so the in-app Upload History stays readable. Re-uploading the same name is safe and additive (it does NOT supersede a prior upload).

default_namespace.description: Optional fallback namespace (SAS libref / SQL schema), UPPERCASE, used only as a hint. Each dataset still declares its own `namespace`.

generated_by.description: Optional label for the tool/agent that produced this extract.

generated_at.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp of when the extract was produced.

datasets.description: Tables/datasets the script reads or writes, with their columns. Optional — but include every table you can describe so new tables/columns are created with real names instead of bare stubs. Existing tables only GAIN new columns; their existing columns and metadata are untouched.

processes.description: The transformation steps (one per DATA step / PROC SQL / CTAS, etc.). Optional but recommended — they carry useful provenance (source file, line range, snippet) and tie inputs to outputs.

table_edges.description: Table-to-table flow edges. Emit one per (input dataset → output dataset) relationship a step establishes.

column_edges.description: Column-to-column lineage edges. Emit one per output column, listing the source column(s) it derives from. This is the highest-value output — be precise.

dataset_id.description: Canonical identity: `<SYSTEM>:<NAMESPACE>.<NAME>` in UPPERCASE (e.g. 'LEGACY:SALES.CUSTOMERS', or 'TARGET:ANALYTICS.PUBLIC.CUSTOMER_SUMMARY' when the namespace itself is dotted like DATABASE.SCHEMA). MUST equal `system + ':' + namespace + '.' + name`. Reuse the EXACT identity of an existing table (from the supplied inventory) to merge into it; otherwise a new table is created.

system.description: Which lane this table lives in. SAS datasets → 'LEGACY'; Snowflake (or other target) structures → 'TARGET'.

namespace.description: SAS libref or SQL schema (DATABASE.SCHEMA), UPPERCASE. Must match the namespace embedded in `dataset_id`.

name.description: Table/dataset name, UPPERCASE. Must match the name embedded in `dataset_id`.

qualified_name.description: UPPERCASE `<NAMESPACE>.<NAME>` (the dataset_id without the system prefix), e.g. 'SALES.CUSTOMERS'.

columns.description: Columns of this table. Optional, but provide every column you can infer (definitions and any column referenced by a column_edge). Existing columns are preserved; only NEW column names are appended (matched case-sensitively against existing UPPERCASE names).

name.description: Column name, UPPERCASE. Must match existing spelling to avoid creating a duplicate column.

data_type.description: Logical/physical type as written in the source (e.g. 'NUMBER', 'VARCHAR', 'CHAR', 'DATE', 'NUMERIC(18,2)'). Use 'UNKNOWN' if the script does not reveal it. Cross-system type equivalence (SAS/Snowflake/PySpark/Pandas) is handled by the app's comparison layer, so do not normalize — record what the source says.

ordinal.description: Optional 1-based column position. Only applied to NEWLY created columns; never reorders existing ones.

process_id.description: Local id for this step, unique within this extract (e.g. 'p1'). Referenced by edges' `process_id`.

operation_type.description: Free-text category of the step, e.g. 'SAS_DATA_STEP', 'SAS_PROC_SQL', 'SAS_PROC_SORT', 'SF_CTAS', 'SQL_INSERT_SELECT'. Be consistent.

source_file.description: The script file this step came from.

inputs.description: dataset_ids this step READS (same `<SYSTEM>:<NAMESPACE>.<NAME>` UPPERCASE format).

sequence.description: Optional execution order within the script (1-based).

name.description: Optional human-readable step name. Defaults to process_id if omitted.

code_location.description: Optional source line range for the step.

description.description: Optional short prose describing what the step does.

snippet.description: Optional verbatim code snippet for the step (keep it short).

edge_id.description: Local id for this edge (e.g. 'te1'). REQUIRED but IGNORED for storage identity — the app derives a content-based id from from_dataset+to_dataset, so re-imports upsert instead of duplicating.

from_dataset.description: Upstream dataset_id (source of the flow), `<SYSTEM>:<NAMESPACE>.<NAME>` UPPERCASE.

to_dataset.description: Downstream dataset_id (target of the flow), same format.

process_id.description: The `process_id` of the step that establishes this flow.

edge_id.description: Local id for this edge (e.g. 'ce1'). REQUIRED but IGNORED for storage identity — the app derives a content-based id from the target column + sorted source columns, so re-imports upsert instead of duplicating.

target.description: The OUTPUT column produced by the step.

sources.description: The INPUT column(s) the target derives from. One entry for a direct/renamed/cast mapping; multiple for expressions/aggregations that combine columns. Use an empty-ish CONSTANT case only when there is genuinely no source — but `sources` must contain at least one entry, so for constants point at the most relevant driving column or omit the edge.

process_id.description: The `process_id` of the step that produces the target column.

transformation_type.description: How the target is derived: DIRECT = copied unchanged; RENAME = copied under a new name; CAST = type conversion only; EXPRESSION = scalar function/arithmetic; AGGREGATION = SUM/COUNT/AVG/etc.; WINDOW = windowed/analytic function; CASE = conditional logic; CONSTANT = literal value; UNKNOWN = cannot determine. Prefer the most specific accurate type; use UNKNOWN rather than guessing.

expression.description: Optional source expression/formula for the derivation (e.g. 'upper(c.cust_name)', 'sum(o.amount)').

confidence.description: Optional confidence in this mapping. HIGH for explicit/direct mappings; MEDIUM/LOW when inferred (e.g. via SELECT * or ambiguous joins).

dataset_id.description: dataset_id of the table this column belongs to, `<SYSTEM>:<NAMESPACE>.<NAME>` UPPERCASE.
